title: Open-LLM structured-output spike

per_script.script: This is Agent Miller with the DEA. We have a warrant for your arrest. You need to verify your identity right now or we will dispatch officers.
per_script.semantic_notes: Missed fear_intimidation; pretexting and urgency are plausible co-occurrences but core label missed

per_script.script: Hello, this is Microsoft support. We detected a virus on your computer. Please go to the start menu and let me guide you through removal.
per_script.semantic_notes: benign is a false positive alongside a malicious label

per_script.script: This is your bank. To continue, please read me the security code we just texted to your phone.
per_script.semantic_notes: Missed credential_harvesting entirely; model treated OTP request as pretexting

per_script.script: Congratulations! You are the lucky winner of a one thousand dollar Walmart gift card. Press 1 now to claim before it expires.
per_script.semantic_notes: reward_prize detected correctly; pretexting and benign are noise

per_script.script: Grandpa, it is me. I am in jail and need bail money right away. Please do not tell mom.
I am calling on behalf of the HR benefits department. We are updating records and need to confirm your social security number.
Your Amazon order for 799 dollars is being processed. If this was not you, please stay on the line to reverse the charge with a refund.
per_script.semantic_notes: Missed financial_manipulation entirely; benign false positive

per_script.script: Hey, it is Mike. Just calling to confirm dinner on Friday at seven. Let me know if that still works for you.
per_script.semantic_notes: Empty array for benign is acceptable — no attack tactics present; benign label absent but not harmful

per_script.script: This is the Social Security Administration. Your number has been suspended due to suspicious activity in Texas.
per_script.semantic_notes: authority correct; fear_intimidation missed; pretexting is noise

per_script.script: Your utility bill is overdue and service will be disconnected in thirty minutes. Pay by card now to avoid disconnection.
per_script.semantic_notes: Both primary labels correct; pretexting is minor noise

semantic_patterns.pretexting_overuse: Used as catch-all in 7/10 scripts including cases where it doesn't apply
semantic_patterns.credential_harvesting_recall: Detected 0/2 times it was clearly present
semantic_patterns.fear_intimidation_recall: Detected 0/2 times it was clearly present
semantic_patterns.benign_false_positive_rate: Added alongside malicious labels in 3/10 scripts
semantic_patterns.benign_on_benign_script: Returned empty array rather than benign label — acceptable but worth noting

prompt_v2_remediation.pretexting: Add explicit note: do NOT use as catch-all; almost always co-occurs with another tactic
prompt_v2_remediation.credential_harvesting: Add targeted few-shot example showing OTP/verification-code request
prompt_v2_remediation.fear_intimidation: Add disambiguation vs urgency: fear = explicit threat; urgency = time pressure only
prompt_v2_remediation.benign: Add rule: use benign ONLY when NO attack tactics are present; never alongside malicious labels

notes: generation flags warning (temperature/top_p/top_k) is cosmetic — do_sample=False ignores them.
evidenceSpans spot-check (verbatim substrings) not captured in this run — manual review needed.
No need to escalate to Qwen 7B or constrained decoding; 3B passes the reliability bar.
